senses.definition: a chemical ester of sebacic acid used in various cosmetics and industrial applications
examples: The formulation includes sebacate as a plasticizer.
Ethyl sebacate is often used as a solvent in coatings.

senses.definition: relating to or producing sebum (oil) from the sebaceous glands in the skin
examples: Sebaceous glands secrete oil to keep the skin moist.
Sebaceous matter can clog pores.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being sebaceous (oily or fatty in nature)
examples: The sebaceousness of the sample gave it a greasy sheen.
Her skin showed obvious sebaceousness in the heat.

senses.definition: relating to sebacic acid, a dicarboxylic acid derived from castor oil
examples: Sebacic acid is used to make lubricants and plastics.
The sebacic compound exhibited high resilience.

senses.definition: a type of large, heavy boat or a brand name; also a place name in Maine
examples: The crew hauled the sebago into the dock.
We visited Sebago Lake during summer.

senses.definition: bait used to attract sebacean or oily targets; note: this term is archaic/nonstandard and rarely used
examples: The fisherman used a thin sebait to lure the fish.
Historical texts mention sebait in coastal trade.

senses.definition: related to sebascic (sebacic) properties; rare usage in modern text
examples: The compound showed sebasic characteristics in tests.

senses.definition: a given name or a surname; also a character name in literature
examples: Sebastian is a common name in many European countries.
Sebastian played the role with quiet intensity.

senses.definition: the name of a character in Shakespeare's Twelfth Night; often used as a literary reference
examples: Sebastian is Viola's twin brother in the play.
Readers recall Sebastian when tracing the shipwreck subplot.

senses.definition: a mineral containing copper and nickel sulfides; a rare mineral name
examples: Sebastianite is listed in mineral catalogs.
The specimen was labeled Sebastianite.

senses.definition: a genus of rockfishes or related marine fishes; a scientific term
examples: Sebastichthys species inhabit rocky shores.
Taxonomists study Sebastichthys in depth.

senses.definition: a rockfish of the genus Sebastodes; a term used in angling or marine biology
examples: A sebastine was spotted near the reef.
The fisher studied the behavior of sebastines.

senses.definition: a genus of rockfishes within the subfamily Sebastinae; taxonomic name
examples: Sebastodes is used in scientific papers on marine life.
Several species of Sebastodes inhabit cold waters.

senses.definition: a historical or linguistic term; may refer to a semitic or local title, varies by context
examples: In old manuscripts, sebat appeared as a title.
The term sebat is rarely used in contemporary English.

senses.definition: to disperse or scatter; to separate during fishing or other activity; rare usage
examples: They sebate the catch into several baskets.
To avoid crowding, they sebate the workers into teams.

senses.definition: a proper noun or surname; rarely used in modern English
examples: Sebates is listed in ancient author catalogs.

senses.definition: a historic or dialect term; sometimes seen as a proper name
examples: SeBesten is referenced in old maritime records.

senses.definition: plural form of a historic or dialect term; may appear in older texts
examples: The term sebestens appears in several nautical logs.

senses.definition: producing or bearing sebum; oily
examples: Sebiferous skin tends to shine in heat.
Sebiferous glands secrete oily substances.

senses.definition: producing sebum or oil; oily in nature
examples: Sebific secretions keep the skin supple.
The sebific tissues responded quickly to the treatment.

senses.definition: an old term for a seat or bench; rarely used
examples: The sebilla in the hall creaked softly.
Historical texts mention sebilla as a place to rest.

senses.definition: producing or bearing sebaceous matter; oily reproductive fluids in some species; rare scientific usage
examples: Sebiparous animals shed oil during mating rituals.

senses.definition: an Arabic term for a wadi or dry streambed; sometimes used in transliterations
examples: The caravan crossed the sebkha at noon.
Sebkha regions are common in arid climates.

senses.definition: a small embryological or fossilized stone; an unusual technical term
examples: The museum displayed a specimen labeled sebolith.

senses.definition: abnormal or excessive flow, typically of sebaceous matter or related substances; medical term
examples: Seborrhagia can be a symptom in certain skin disorders.
Treatment targets the seborrhagic symptoms.

senses.definition: excessive discharge of sebum from the sebaceous glands; oily skin condition
examples: Seborrhea often causes greasy hair and skin.
Proper skincare can help manage seborrhea.
